title: Listening
content:

    That hurtles on the window pane.
    Let's draw the curtains close and sit

title: Luggage in Advance
content:

title: To Mother
content:
I would that you should know,
    Dear mother, that I love you -- love you so!
    That I remember other days and years;

title: Two Women
content:

title: God save the King
content:
GOD SAVE OUR GRACIOUS KING. (It seems
    The Church is full of bygone dreams.)

    LONG LIVE OUR NOBLE KING. (My own,
    'Tis hard to stand

title: The Housewife
content:
See, I am cumbered, Lord,
    With serving, and with small vexatious things.
    Upstairs, and down, my feet
    Must hasten, sure and fleet.

title: Song of a Woodland Stream
content:
Silent was I, and so still,
    As day followed day.
    Imprisoned until
    King Frost worked his will.
    Held fast like a vice,
    In h

title: Summer met Me
content:
Summer met me in the glade,
    With a host of fair princesses,
    Golden iris, foxgloves staid,
    Sunbeams flecked their gorgeous dresses.

title: The Carrier
content:
"Owd John's got past his work," said they,
    Last week as ever was -- "don't pay
    To send by him. He's stoopid, too,
    And brings things

title: The Flight of the Fairies
A Woman in Hospital
content:

    For I am God. I am Jehovah, He
    Who made you what you are; and I can see

title: At the Cross Roads
content:
There I halted. Further down the hollow
    Stood the township, where my errand lay.

title: In Convalescence
content:
Not long ago, I prayed for dying grace,
    For then I thought to see Thee face to face.

title: To a Little White Bird
content:
Into the world you came, and I was dumb,
    Because "God did it," so the wise ones said;
    I wonder sometimes "Did you really come?"

title: When Baby Strayed
content:
When Baby strayed, it seemed to me,

title: The Witness
content:

title: The Daily Interview
The Log Fire
content:
In her last hour of life the tree
    Gave up her glorious memories,
    Wild scent of wood anemone,
    The sapphire blue of April skies.

title: The Street Player
content:
The shopping had been tedious, and the rain
    Came pelting down as she turned home again.

    The motor-bus swirled past with rush and whirr,

title: The Long View
content:
Some day of days! Some dawning yet to be
    I shall be clothed with immortality!

    And, in that day, I shall not greatly care
    That Jane

title: The Dear Folks in Devon
content:
Back in the dear old country 'tis Christ- mas, and to-night
    I'm thinking of the mistletoe and holly berries bright.

title: The Little House
content:

title: When He Comes
content:
"When He comes!
    My sweetest 'When'!"
             C. ROSSETTI.

    Thus may it be (I thought) at some day's close,

title: In Such an Hour
content:
Sometimes, when everything goes wrong:
    When days are short, and nights are long;
    When wash-day brings so dull a sky

title: Little Fan
content:
When little Fanny came to town, I felt as I could sing!
    She were the sprackest little maid, the sharpest, pertest thing.
    Her mother were

title: The Naughty Day
content:
I've had a naughty day to-day.
    I scrunched a biscuit in my hair,
    And dipped my feeder in the milk,
    And spread my rusk upon a chair.

title: For Mothering!
content:

    For little Miss and Master'll be coming down from town.

title: The Reason
content:
"Why shouldest Thou be as a wayfaring man, that turneth aside to tarry for a night?" -- Jer. xiv. 8.

title: Within my House
The Home Lights
content:
"In my father's house!" The words
    Bring sweet cadence to my ears.
    Wandering thoughts, like homing birds,
    Fly all swiftly down the y

title: Early Spring
content:
Quick through the gates of Fairyland
    The South Wind forced his way.
    'Twas his to make the Earth forget
    Her grief of yesterday.

title: In Dorset Dear
content:
In Dorset Dear they're making hay
    In just the old West Country way.
    With fork and rake and old-time gear
    They make the hay in Dorse

title: The Prize Fight
content:

title: In Somerset
content:

title: To an Old Teapot
content:
Now from the dust of half-forgotten things,

title: The Thrush
content:

content:

content:

title: Homesick
content:

    An' back among the Cotswolds I were wanderin' in me dreams.

title: On All Souls' Eve
content:

title: The Lad's Love by the Gate
content:
